name: Clarence Muse
abstract: Clarence Muse  (October 14, 1889 – October 13, 1979) was an American actor, screenwriter, director, singer, and composer. He was the first African American to appear in a starring role in a major studio film, 1929's Hearts in Dixie.

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: I think Clarence Muse (INSONIC) got in on that, but they were, I think, the real writers of it, you know. But Clarence did it and featured it and, you know, sold it for them.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Muse was trying to get me a produ...somebody to produce it. ... I went over to see Clarence ... we played the song ... there's a picture of you with Clarence Muse.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, him and Clarence Muse. ... Otis and Clarence Muse wrote... I think Clarence Muse was the co-writer on 'When The Swallows Come Back To Capistrano.'

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I went with the Clarence Muse Show. Clarence Muse and the Charleston Dandies, that was the name of the show.

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: Clarence Muse put it in the Clarence Muse biography about him being a lawyer... So he put the lawyer down.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernest 'Punch' Miller
about_quotes.evidence: Clarence Muse, the man in pictures, he was on the show with us.
